market_context: H2 (2025-11-22 to 2026-05-21) was defined by three sequential macro regimes that rewarded cross-period thematic positioning rather than single-catalyst day-trades. Regime 1 (Nov–Jan): Risk-on post-election euphoria drove AI momentum stocks (PLTR, software) to extreme valuations while simultaneously igniting a structural AI-storage supercycle as hyperscalers committed hundreds of billions to data center buildout — HDD/memory names like SNDK, STX, WDC began a multi-month re-rating. NATO defense spending pledges (5% GDP by 2035) drove a parallel re-rating in defense primes (LMT, NOC, RTX), with ITA gaining ~55% YoY. Gold broke out on central-bank buying + debasement fears, with GDX miners leveraging physical gold gains 2-3x. Regime 2 (Feb–Mar): Valuation gravity hit extended tech names (PLTR -32% from Dec peak), while gold/miners reached local peaks around $4,961/oz (Jan 23) before a sharp March correction. S&P 500 additions created mechanical buying windows: CVNA (Dec 22 effective), VRT/LITE/COHR (Mar 23 effective). Regime 3 (Apr–May): 'Liberation Day' tariffs (Apr 2) briefly crashed markets — SPY touched multi-month lows — but the Apr 9 tariff pause triggered the largest single-day rally in years. AI storage stocks continued their supercycle regardless of tariff noise: STX +243%, SNDK +680% from H2 start to mid-May. Defense stocks peaked in March (LMT hit $671) then sold off as Ukraine-NATO peace talks cooled near-term order expectations. SPY turned positive for 2026 on May 13. The dominant cross-period alpha came from structural positioning in AI storage infrastructure and tactical fades of euphoric AI software valuations.

trades.thesis: SanDisk (Western Digital spinoff) is the single highest-beta AI storage play: newly independent, pure-play NAND/flash, with structural demand from AI inference and data lakes. As AI hyperscalers committed to record capex, NAND supply-demand imbalance became structural — Micron stated publicly it was sold out through all of 2026. SNDK as a pure-play spinoff carried fresh institutional underownership and suffered from a lower starting valuation multiple, making it the highest-conviction leverage vehicle on the AI storage supercycle. The multi-month holding captures the full re-rating from spinoff discount to recognized AI infrastructure play.
trades.catalyst: Structural: AI inference requires exponentially more storage than training. Tactical: WDC spinoff completion (SNDK newly independent) creates institutional underownership. S&P 500 inclusion of memory/storage sector anticipated. Micron 'sold out through 2026' public statement in late 2025 confirms supply tightness.
trades.lesson: Pure-play spinoffs on structural secular themes are the highest-beta expression of the thesis. The AI storage supercycle was foreseeable from supply constraint data (Micron sold-out commentary, HDD HAMR ramp) in late 2025 — holding through 5-month tariff and macro noise was the key behavioral discipline required.

trades.thesis: Seagate is the premier HDD manufacturer with HAMR (Heat-Assisted Magnetic Recording) technology entering volume production in late 2025, enabling Mozaic 3+ drives with record 40.1% gross margins. AI data lakes and inference workloads create secular demand for mass-capacity HDD — a structurally different market than flash. With near-line HDD lead times extending and cloud providers over-ordering, STX is in a multi-year upcycle. Lower valuation than SNDK makes it a complementary position. Holding period: full H2 period to capture both the fundamental re-rating and potential index additions.
trades.catalyst: HAMR technology volume ramp (late 2025), AI inference storage demand, hyperscaler capex commitments, supply-demand imbalance in near-line HDD.
trades.lesson: Pairs trade with SNDK works — STX the 'safer' HDD play vs SNDK pure-play NAND. Both captured the supercycle. The key insight: AI infrastructure spending eventually flows downstream from compute (NVDA) to storage (STX/SNDK/WDC) with a 6-12 month lag, creating a predictable wave trade.

trades.thesis: Gold miners (GDX) provide 2-3x leveraged exposure to physical gold via operational leverage — fixed costs mean incremental gold price gains flow almost entirely to profits. With gold at record highs driven by (1) central bank diversification away from USD, (2) Trump tariff-driven debasement fears, (3) geopolitical risk premium, the GDX/GLD ratio was breaking out of a 15-year underperformance base. Miners had lagged physical gold for years (historically GDX returned 108% vs gold's 52% over the following year). Entry at Nov 30 after the initial November pullback; exit at the March 1 peak before the sharp mid-March correction.
trades.catalyst: Gold hit $4,961/oz record (Jan 23, 2026). Trump 25% tariff announcement created debasement hedge bid. Central bank buying hit multi-year highs. GDX/GLD ratio technical breakout from 15-year base.
trades.lesson: Operational leverage makes miners a superior vehicle to physical gold in a bull market — fixed cost base means margins expand dramatically with gold price. The key timing discipline: exit before March 8-22 selloff (GDX dropped from 115 to 80) by watching the GLD/Gold spot peak. Miners should be sold before gold rolls.

trades.thesis: Palantir hit an all-time high of $207.18 on Nov 3, 2025 and a near-peak of $193.98 by Dec 22, trading at 154x trailing P/E and >40x forward sales. This is a classic euphoric overshoot: 15/15 sell-side analysts bullish (Buwen framework: 15/15 = priced in), retail FOMO at peak, and the stock up 107%+ YTD going into year-end. While PLTR's AI fundamentals (AIP adoption, U.S. commercial +137% YoY) are genuine, the valuation implies perfection with no margin for miss. Historical analog: high-P/E AI software names corrected 25-40% in early 2026 as rate expectations were recalibrated. Short from the Dec euphoria peak into the Feb earnings hangover.
trades.catalyst: Euphoric valuation (154x P/E), crowded positioning, year-end momentum exhaustion, institutional profit-taking in Jan, no major positive catalyst between Dec peak and Q4 earnings (Q4 reported ~Feb 2026).
trades.lesson: Shorting crowded AI software at peak euphoria (15/15 consensus + 100%+ YTD run) works over 6-8 week windows even when fundamentals are strong. The distinction: PLTR's business is excellent; the stock was still a short at 154x P/E. Valuation discipline > narrative. Cover before Q1 2026 earnings (PLTR rebounded from ~$131 toward $160 in March on strong results).

trades.thesis: Mechanical S&P 500 inclusion trade. On Dec 5, 2025, S&P Dow Jones announced CVNA would join the S&P 500 effective Dec 22, 2025. Passive index funds (managing ~$8-10T tracking S&P 500) must purchase approximately 16 million CVNA shares before the Dec 22 open. This creates predictable buying pressure in the 17-day window between announcement and effective date. CVNA was already on a strong fundamental recovery trajectory (100 billion market cap turnaround story), and short interest was elevated — forced short covering amplifies the move. Classic announcement-to-effective-date compression trade.
trades.catalyst: S&P 500 inclusion announcement Dec 5, 2025; effective date Dec 22, 2025; passive fund rebalancing (~16M shares to buy); elevated short interest creating squeeze dynamics.
trades.lesson: S&P 500 inclusion trades (announcement day buy, effective date sell) are one of the most reliable mechanical trades in US equities — the academic literature documents consistent +8-15% average returns in this window due to forced passive buying. The edge is diminishing as the trade gets crowded, but still works especially for mid-cap names joining a large index. Exit ON the effective date, not after.

trades.thesis: Lockheed Martin is the primary beneficiary of the secular defense spending supercycle. NATO allies committed to 5% GDP defense spending by 2035 (Hague declaration), Canada announced its largest defense increase since WWII, and Trump called for a $1.5T U.S. defense budget (FY2027). LMT backlog and revenue visibility are exceptional: $77.5-80B FY2026 sales guidance, $6.5-6.8B FCF. The stock was undervalued at $455 entry (Nov 22) relative to the new defense spending paradigm. Multi-month holding from Nov 22 through the NATO/Ukraine headlines peak, exiting before the late-April selloff as Ukraine peace talks cooled demand expectations.
trades.catalyst: NATO 5% GDP pledge, Trump $1.5T defense budget request, Canada defense spending increase, geopolitical risk premium (Russia-Ukraine), F-35 production ramp, elevated backlog.
trades.lesson: Defense primes are structural buys when NATO spending commitments become binding — the political decision precedes the earnings impact by 2-4 years but the stock re-rates immediately. Exit signal: LMT fell from $671 (Mar 8) to $506 (May 10) as Ukraine peace negotiations raised questions about European defense order deceleration. The Zelenskyy NATO-bid withdrawal (Dec 15) should have been watched as a risk signal — it briefly dipped defense stocks and foreshadowed the later selloff.

trades.thesis: Vertiv (VRT) is the dominant provider of power and cooling infrastructure for AI data centers. Added to S&P 500 on March 23, 2026 (announced March 6) — mechanical inclusion buying plus fundamental momentum from AI capex cycle. VRT was already in a strong uptrend on data center thermal management demand (AI chips run hotter, requiring liquid cooling) and had just announced record backlogs. Entry on March 8 (pullback day after March 6 announcement spike fades), holding through the S&P 500 inclusion and the tariff-resilient AI infrastructure buildout into May. VRT is non-China-tariff-exposed (manufactures domestically and in Europe), making it a Liberation Day-resistant holding.
trades.catalyst: S&P 500 addition announced March 6 (effective March 23), AI data center power/cooling structural demand, tariff-resilient supply chain (not exposed to China import tariffs), hyperscaler capex commitments to liquid-cooled AI infrastructure.
trades.lesson: VRT illustrates the compound trade: buy on S&P 500 addition announcement (mechanical buying), hold for the fundamental AI infrastructure re-rating. The best S&P 500 inclusion trades are the ones where the company also has genuine fundamental momentum — the index inclusion is the catalyst to enter, but the business is the reason to hold. VRT's tariff immunity (domestic/European manufacturing) was key to holding through Liberation Day without forced exit.

period_summary.regime_notes: H2 2025-11-22 to 2026-05-21 featured three macro regimes. Regime 1 (Nov-Jan): AI euphoria + defense re-rating + gold breakout — multi-week trend longs in LMT, STX, SNDK, GDX were the core playbook. Regime 2 (Jan-Mar): Valuation gravity hit crowded AI software — PLTR short paid. S&P 500 addition mechanics created tactical windows (CVNA Dec, VRT Mar). Regime 3 (Apr-May): Liberation Day tariff shock was a one-day event; AI storage supercycle (SNDK, STX, WDC, MU) was the dominant force and absorbed the macro noise entirely. Cross-period insight: structural themes (AI storage supercycle, NATO defense spending, gold debasement hedge) outperformed all single-catalyst plays. The highest-alpha trade (SNDK +680%) required a 24-week hold through multiple macro shocks — behavioral discipline (not selling into volatility) was the actual edge, not the original idea.
